summarization.a: 3D scatter plot of kMC simulation results for Step B (TMA etching) showing half-cycle time as a function of temperature (533-598 K) and pressure (10-100 Pa). The data reveals strong dependencies on both parameters: half-cycle times range from approximately 2 s at high temperature/pressure to over 20 s at low temperature. This dataset was used to train the FNN model for Step B.

a.question: How do temperature and pressure each affect the Step B half-cycle time?
a.answer: Both parameters significantly influence Step B half-cycle time. Temperature has the dominant effect: at low temperatures (~533 K), times exceed 20 s, while at high temperatures (~598 K), times drop to around 2-3 s, reflecting Arrhenius kinetics. Pressure also matters for Step B because TMA adsorption kinetics depend on partial pressure. Higher pressures accelerate the secondary TMA adsorption step, reducing half-cycle time. This dual dependence contrasts with Step A, which is largely pressure-independent.

a.question: How were these data points generated?
a.answer: Data points were generated by running kMC simulations across temperature (533-598 K in 5 K intervals) and pressure (10-100 Pa in 5 Pa intervals), averaging 10 simulations per condition to reduce stochastic variation.

a.question: Why does generating this dataset require substantial computational resources?
a.answer: Each kMC simulation takes approximately 20 minutes on average, and multiple simulations are averaged per operating condition to account for the inherent randomness of the Monte Carlo method.

a.question: What do the three axes in this plot represent?
a.answer: Temperature in Kelvin (x-axis), Pressure in Pascals (y-axis), and half-cycle time in seconds (z-axis).
